hub.title: Body composition
hub.subtitle: Choose which tool you want to use
hub.calculateDesc: Measure and calculate your body fat percentage and lean mass using various methods such as calipers and tape measure
hub.geneticDesc: Estimate your maximum genetic muscle potential based on skeletal structure and body measurements
hub.missingDataAlert: To see your personal values you need to enter body fat %, weight and height in your profile.
hub.ffmiModalSubtitle: Measure of muscle mass relative to height

results.estimatedRMR: Based on estimated RMR from the Mifflin-St Jeor equation

list.unsavedWarning: You have unsaved changes. Do you want to continue? Changes will be lost.
list.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the measurement set from {{date}} - {{time}}? This cannot be undone.
list.emptyList: No saved measurements yet. Fill in measurements and save!

caliper.title: Caliper measurements
caliper.description: Skinfold measurements are taken on the right side of the body. Make sure the skinfold is grasped correctly with thumb and forefinger, without pulling in muscle tissue. The caliper should be placed perpendicular to the skinfold, about 1 cm from the fingers, and the reading should be taken after 1–2 seconds when the pressure has stabilized. Take each skinfold at least twice and use the average. If the difference is >1–2 mm, take a third measurement. Entered in millimeters (mm).

tape.title: Tape measurements
tape.description: Measure circumference at indicated points with a tape measure. Measure while standing relaxed and breathing normally. Make sure the tape measure is not too tight or too loose and that it lies flat against the skin. Entered in centimeters (cm).

methodModal.returnsDensityNote: - Then converted to body fat % using the Siri or Brozek equation.

ffmiCard.noHeightMessage: Height information missing from profile to calculate FFMI.

maxFat.title: Maximum fat metabolism
maxFat.description: This represents the maximum rate at which your body can metabolize fat for energy (approximately 31 kcal/kg of fat mass per day). Exceeding this deficit may result in muscle loss.
